figures.caption: Figure 2 | Noradrenergic neurons are directly responsive to brain pO2 changes. 1144 a, Schematic diagram showing the locations of the four groups of noradrenergic neurons. The 1145 boxed region is shown in (b). 1146
Figure 1 | Larval zebrafish show oxygen-dependent changes in behavior and brain-wide 1076 calcium dynamics. 1077

Figure 3 | Predictive regulation of oxygen use by noradrenergic-astroglial signaling. 1200 a, Whole-cell recordings of NE-MO subthreshold voltage activity with simultaneous fictive 1201 swimming. Right, swim-triggered EPSPs for one cell across 60 swim bouts; mean ± s.e.m. is 1202 shown. 1203

Figure 4 | Noradrenergic-astroglial signaling is necessary for hypoxia-induced brain 1267 dynamics and coping behavior. 1268 a, Schematic showing two-photon laser ablation of NE-MO neurons. 1269 b,c, Example activity matrices showing the change of neuronal (b) and astroglial (c) cellular fast 1270 calcium (ΔF/F) responses to hypoxia before and after NE-MO ablation. Both matrices were 1271 clustered using factor analysis of the fast calcium component before NE-MO ablation. The spatial 1272 location and temporal responses for each cluster are shown in Extended Data Figure 6f,g. Bottom 1273 black traces show fictive swimming. 1274
